title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: LCFS Credit Prices Hold Firm Amid Robust Trading Activity
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights:

(25th November â€“ 1st December)
  • A total of 289,581credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a rise of 9.80% week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades increased by 69.8% WoW as 685,531credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price increased slightly this week to $76.63/MTÂ for Type 1Â transfers, recording a 0.50%Â rise WoW; All Non-Zero transfers declined by 3.47% in price, reaching $70.07/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price increased marginally by 1% WoW; on 3rd Dec, it was $78.13/MT. The benchmark price stood at $79.00/MT on 3rd
  • D3 RIN prices fell by 5% WoW, while D4, D5, and D6 RIN prices each fell by 6.66% WoW.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

The California LCFS market saw robust activity this week, with Type 1 trades rising by 9.80% WoW to 289,581 credits, contributing to a 69.8% increase in total non-zero trades, which reached 685,531 credits. The average credit price for Type 1 transfers edged up by 0.50% to $76.63/MT, while non-zero trades saw a price decline of 3.47%, settling at $70.07/MT. Spot credit prices showed stability, rising marginally by 1% to $78.13/MT, close to the benchmark price of $79.00/MT. The gradual price increase in Type 1 trades and spot credits indicates tightening supply conditions as market participants prepare for changes in credit availability under the amended regulations. The updates, which include stricter CI benchmarks, expanded feedstock pathways, and accelerated ZEV adoption targets, are expected to impact credit supply. This has led to a focus on compliance planning and feedstock diversification as stakeholders adjust to the upcoming changes, ensuring readiness for the new regulatory framework.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

  Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB The average Type 1 prices recorded a 0.50% WoW rise, increasing from $76.26 to $76.63 this week, while all non-zero credit prices declined by 3.47% WoW, dropping from $72.56 to $70.07. Type 1 trades saw 289,581 credits exchanged, reflecting a 9.80% WoW increase. Similarly, the total transaction volume for all non-zero trades surged by 69.8% WoW to reach 685,531 credits.

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info) Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices increased marginally by 1% WoW, reaching $78.13/MT. On 3rd December, the average spot price was $78.13/MT, a rise of 1% WoW, while the benchmark, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2024, stood at $79.00/MT as of 3rd December.

CA LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB As per the data reported by CARB last week, 36 trades were reported in the market, with eleven high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was at $78.00 for two Type 1 transactions, with one transaction having the highest volume of 10,000 credits. Last week, the lowest transfer by price was $50.80 per credit of Type 2, reported at a volume of 50,000 credits. The smallest volume transfers ofÂ  100 credits of Type 1 category, were undertaken for price of $76.05.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB November 2024 shows a decline in trading volume, with prices still increasing. November volumes traded have been significantly lower than the preceding month. While October witnessed record volumes of 6.4 MMT, only 2.01MMT of volume was recorded in the month of November. Average Type 1 prices for November stood at $73.07/MT, higher than the average of $65.93/MT observed in October.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 25th Â Â Nov â€“ 1st Dec, total VPT recorded a Â 6.80% decline (WoW), with all non-zero prices falling over the previous week.

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN weekly price trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices fell by 5% WoW and D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices fell by 6.66% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming dates

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments this week

Governor Newsom Commits to Restarting Stateâ€™s ZEV Rebate Program if Federal Tax Credit is Eliminated California has surpassed 2 million zero-emission vehicles (ZEV) sold, reaffirming Californiaâ€™s status as the countryâ€™s leader in clean vehicles. This milestone comes a little over two years after California eclipsed the 1 million ZEV sales mark. To ensure the stateâ€™s continued progress, Governor Gavin Newsom today announced that if the incoming Trump Administration eliminates a federal ZEV tax credit, he will propose creating a new version of the stateâ€™s successful Clean Vehicle Rebate Program, which was phased out in 2023. During its lifetime, the CVRP funded more than 594,000 vehicles and saved more than 456 million gallons of fuel. LPO Announces Conditional Commitment to Rivian to Support the Construction of EV Manufacturing Facility in Georgia The U.S. Department of Energy's Loan Programs Office (LPO) announced a conditional commitment to Rivian New Horizon, LLC, for a direct loan of up to $6.57 billion. This funding will support the development and construction of Project Horizon, an electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, Georgia, which will produce mid-sized electric SUVs. The project aims to create up to 9,500 jobs by 2030 and manufacture up to 400,000 vehicles annually, contributing to the Biden-Harris Administrationâ€™s goal of having half of all new vehicles sold by 2030 be zero-emissions. This loan is the first under the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) Loan Program during the current administration. Biden-Harris Administration Announces Nearly $71 Million to Continue Strengthening America's Auto Communities, Expanding Battery Recycling, and Modernizing Manufacturing As part of President Biden's Investing in America agenda, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced $70.8 million to support partnerships with state and local governments, aimed at assisting small- and medium-sized manufacturers in sectors like automotive, battery recycling, and smart manufacturing. The funding will help upgrade manufacturing facilities, improve battery recycling, and modernize state-level manufacturing, with a focus on benefiting disadvantaged communities. This includes $50 million for retooling auto facilities in five states, $11.5 million to boost battery recycling efforts in seven states, and $9.3 million to accelerate smart manufacturing adoption across five states. The initiative supports the Biden-Harris Administrationâ€™s goals of strengthening domestic manufacturing and promoting clean energy. U.S. Department of Transportation Launches Climate Action Strategy Playbook to Help Communities Across America Reduce Emissions The U.S. Department of Transportation has released the "Climate Strategies That Work" playbook, featuring 28 research-based strategies to help State, Tribal, city, and local governments reduce carbon emissions and build sustainable transportation systems. Aligned with the U.S. National Blueprint for Transportation Decarbonization, the playbook covers solutions like active transportation, EVs, and freight efficiency, offering case studies, funding guidance, and insights on achieving greenhouse gas reductions, economic growth, and safety. It aims to empower local leaders with data-driven tools for creating efficient, clean, and accessible transportation systems. Washigton Clean Fuel Standard Rulemaking Update On November 26, 2024, the Washington State Department of Ecology released updated draft rules for the Clean Fuel Standard. These revisions address accounting methods, methane crediting, hydrogen use, fuel pathways, and zero-emission vehicle infrastructure credits. The full draft text, highlighting changes since August, is available for public review and comment.
